## Task: Fix [high] UI/UX: Dropdown menu positioned at raw click coords overflows the viewport near edges
## Step: review-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: high
File: src/app/dom-builders.ts:225-236
Title: Dropdown menu positioned at raw click coords overflows the viewport near edges

Finding:
openMenu(x, y) sets `menu.style.left = x + 'px'; menu.style.top = y + 'px'` with position:fixed and no clamping. When opened via right-click near the right or bottom edge of the viewport (or via the button when the row is at the bottom), the 140px-min-width menu overflows off-screen, gets clipped, and/or introduces a horizontal/vertical scrollbar — making action items (especially Delete/Copy on the right edge) partially or fully invisible and unclickable. Standard dropdown behavior flips/clamps the menu to stay within the viewport.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/dom-builders.ts, in the `openMenu(x, y)` function inside makeRowMenu, after setting menu.hidden = false (so it has a measurable size) and before/after setting left/top, clamp the coordinates to the viewport. Read the menu's rendered size and the viewport, e.g.:

const rect = menu.getBoundingClientRect();
const maxX = window.innerWidth - rect.width - 4;
const maxY = window.innerHeight - rect.height - 4;
menu.style.left = Math.max(4, Math.min(x, maxX)) + 'px';
menu.style.top = Math.max(4, Math.min(y, maxY)) + 'px';

Make sure to read getBoundingClientRect() AFTER revealing the menu (menu.hidden = false) so width/height are non-zero. Keep the position:fixed styling and the existing close-on-document-click/Escape behavior unchanged.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The test file `src/app/render-browse.test.ts` contains a well-designed `describe('viewport clamping (overflow near edges)')` section with 8 tests that correctly verify the expected clamping behavior. The tests are specific, focused, cover edge cases (negative coords, far-right/bottom overflow, mixed axis overflow, range of edge coordinates), follow existing patterns, and are independent. Helper functions (`expectedClampX`, `expectedClampY`, `px`, `openViaContext`) are appropriately used. However, all 8 viewport clamping tests are FAILING because the implementation in `src/app/dom-builders.ts` has not yet added the viewport clamping logic to the `openMenu(x, y)` function — the function still sets `menu.style.left = x + 'px'` and `menu.style.top = y + 'px'` without any clamping. The test file also includes many additional new test sections (scroll/resize dismissal, ARIA menu pattern, accessibility header) that are not directly related to the viewport clamping task but accompany other changes in `dom-builders.ts`. Some of those additional tests are also failing (scroll/resize dismissal, accessibility header), indicating incomplete implementation for those features as well.
